invariantResults.evidence: All 9 headings use sentence case correctly: 'View member details', 'External user accounts', 'Disable or delete members', 'Member roles', 'View member roles', 'Create a member role', 'Edit a member role', 'Assign members to roles', 'Next steps'.
Verb-phrase headings use imperative mood: 'View member details', 'Disable or delete members', 'Create a member role', 'Edit a member role', 'Assign members to roles'.
No heading is prefixed with 'How to'.
Title 'Members' and all subheadings are unambiguous. Subheadings scoped under clear parent sections.
Title 'Members' and headings like 'Member roles', 'Create a member role' use searchable terms a business user would look for.
Feature names consistently capitalized: 'Members' application (bolded throughout), 'Page Builder' not referenced. 'Members' used consistently as the application name.

Active voice used throughout procedures. Line 15 uses 'are managed' (passive) which falls under allowed exception (b) — emphasizing the receiver ('Member accounts and member roles'). Line 31 'is allowed' describes a field's function, not an instruction.
Present simple tense used throughout. No instances of 'will be', 'would be', or 'shall'.
American English throughout. No British spellings found (e.g., uses 'organize' not 'organise').

Consistent terminology: 'member' (never 'subscriber' or 'user' for members), 'member role' (never 'group' or 'permission'), 'Members application' (consistent bolding). 'Membership role name' used consistently for the field.

Line 21: 'it doesn't support' — 'it' refers to 'your project' from earlier in the same sentence, antecedent is clear within the same sentence. Line 50: 'removes it from the system' and 'It cannot be used' — 'it' refers to 'member account' from the same sentence. Pronouns stay within their paragraphs with clear antecedents.
All instructions use imperative mood ('Open the Members application', 'Select New membership role', 'Enter a Membership role name'). Descriptive text uses indicative mood.
Gender-neutral language throughout. Uses 'their' (line 27: 'their account details'), 'the member', 'the visitor'. No gendered pronouns.
No culturally specific idioms, slang, holiday references, or sports metaphors.

Steps 1-2 ('Open Members application / Go to List of membership roles tab') repeat across View/Create/Edit role sections, but this is correct per INV-structure-026 and INV-style-025 — each section must be standalone.
No verbose phrases found. Language is direct throughout.
No filler words ('actually', 'basically', 'really', 'just', 'in fact') found.

Most sentences are under 20 words. Lines 13, 61, 63, 67 have longer sentences but these are introductory/contextual paragraphs where density is acceptable. All procedural steps are concise single-action sentences.
Specific language throughout: concrete UI labels ('List of membership roles', 'New membership role', 'Membership role name'), specific examples ('Subscribers', 'Premium members').
No instances of the word 'please'.
Content is concise and gets to the point. Introduction immediately explains what members are, procedures start directly with steps.
Simple English throughout. No complex vocabulary or convoluted sentence structures.
No '(s)' optional plurals found.

Conditions lead instructions: 'To disable an account:' (line 43), 'To delete an account:' (line 52), 'To see the member roles...' (line 73), 'To create a new member role:' (line 84), 'To change the details...' (line 100), 'To assign a member...' (line 112), 'To remove a member...' (line 123).

Page is fully self-contained. Introduction defines members vs users. Links to Registration and authentication, Secure pages, Secure content items, and Member roles (developer). No assumed prior reading.
Title 'Members' does not end with -ing.

'Select' used correctly for all UI interactions: 'Select a member' (line 27), 'select the additional actions menu' (line 45), 'Select Disable' (line 46), 'Select Delete' (line 54), 'Select New membership role' (line 88), 'Select Save' (lines 92, 106, 117). No instances of 'Click'.
N/A — no checkbox clearing instructions in this page.
'Enter' used correctly for typing values: 'Enter a Membership role name' (line 89), 'Enter a Code name' (line 90). No instances of 'Type' for input actions.
'Open' used correctly for the Members application: lines 75, 86, 102, 114, 123. Consistent and correct.
PREVIOUSLY FAILED — NOW FIXED. All tab navigation uses 'Go to': 'Go to the List of membership roles tab' on lines 76, 87, 103. Zero instances of 'Switch to' remain. Verified via full-text search.
No instances of 'Press'. No keyboard interactions described on this page.
No instances of 'Click'. All UI interactions use 'Select'.

Line 31: 'Enabled' is a field label description, not an instruction to enable a UI element. Line 46: 'Select Disable' refers to the Disable button/action label. Both are acceptable uses — the rule prohibits instructing users to 'Enable X' or 'Disable X' for specific UI elements like checkboxes.
No instances of 'Check' or 'Uncheck'.
PREVIOUSLY FAILED — NOW FIXED. Zero instances of 'Choose'. The rewritten 'Assign members to roles' section (lines 110-123) uses 'select' exclusively. Verified via full-text search.

Instructions are action-focused: 'Enter a Membership role name' (not 'Type in the Membership role name field'), 'Select Save' (not 'Select the Save button'). Line 91 uses 'add a Description' which is action-focused.
No instances of 'you must', 'you should', or 'you could'. Line 67 uses 'must' but directs at 'Your development team', not the reader as an individual, and describes a technical requirement.

Line 67: 'Your development team must also implement' — addresses the team, not the reader directly. Acceptable per the rule that 'must' is fine when not writing about people directly. Minor stretch but acceptable in context of an info callout directing to the dev team.
No instances of 'have to'.
Line 21 uses 'doesn't' — a common contraction contributing to friendly tone.
No awkward contractions ('there'd', 'it'll', 'they'd').
No double contractions.
No noun+verb contractions (e.g., no 'Kentico's developing').
Contractions used sparingly and consistently. Only 'doesn't' on line 21. Rest of document uses spelled-out forms. No mixing within the same passage.
N/A — no navigation paths expressed in arrow notation. The page correctly uses numbered step-by-step format for procedures, which is appropriate for how-to instructions.
N/A — no arrow notation used.
Only abbreviation used is 'e.g.' (lines 37, 89) — well-known abbreviation.
Line 45 uses '(...)' to refer to the additional actions menu — this is the expand box / overflow menu label exception explicitly allowed by the rule.
No possessive constructions of inanimate objects. Uses 'the role's Membership role name' (line 105) which is acceptable as a natural possessive for a named entity.
All required elements present: Title ('Members', line 2), Introduction (lines 13-23), Body (lines 25-123 with multiple sections), Result statements after each procedure, Next Steps (lines 125-129).

Introduction (lines 13-16) provides context (what members are, how they differ from users), real-world scenario (browsing pages, member-only sections), and value (manage accounts and roles via the Members application).
Introduction answers: What? (members are registered visitors), Why? (manage access to front-facing application), When? (when you need to manage member accounts and role-based content access).

Page is moderately long but each section is self-contained with clear headings. A Key Points box could enhance scannability but is not strictly required. The info callout on line 19 and the member roles info callout on line 65 serve a similar orienting purpose.

All step-by-step procedures use ordered lists (Disable: lines 45-46, Delete: lines 54-55, View roles: 75-76, Create role: 86-92, Edit role: 102-106, Assign members: 114-117). Unordered lists used for field descriptions (lines 29-31) and Next Steps (lines 127-129) where order does not matter.

All steps contain one action each. No compound steps with 'and then' or multiple actions. Step 4 in Edit (line 105: 'Update the role's Membership role name, Code name, or Description as needed') lists alternatives within one update action, which is a single conceptual action.
Step counts: Disable (2), Delete (2), View roles (2), Create role (7), Edit role (5), Assign members (4). All under 8. Largest procedure (Create role) has 7 steps.

End results described: 'The member account is now disabled...' (line 48), 'The member account is now deleted' (line 57), role listing description (line 78), 'The new member role is now available...' (line 96), 'The member role is now updated' (line 108), 'The member is now part of the selected roles...' (line 121).
Next Steps section (lines 125-129) links to three related articles: Secure pages, Secure content items, and Member roles (developer docs).
Article is self-contained. Introduction defines members, distinguishes from users, explains the Members application. All referenced concepts are linked. No assumed prior reading.
Procedures assume happy path — user has access to the Members application and the project has members. Prerequisites mentioned contextually (info callout about empty application, line 19-23).

No redundant introduction sentences. Heading 'Create a member role' is followed directly by 'To create a new member role:' which provides the lead-in, not a redundant restatement. Similarly for other sections.
Headings use general terms: 'View member details', 'Disable or delete members', 'Member roles', 'Assign members to roles'. No Kentico-specific jargon in headings.
All list items start with sentence case. Field descriptions (lines 29-31) start with bold field names (capitalized). Next steps items start with capitalized verbs ('Learn', 'Restrict', 'For').
Ordered lists for procedures, unordered lists for field descriptions and next steps. Screenshots break up text sections. No walls of text.
Task-oriented throughout: 'View member details', 'Disable or delete members', 'Create a member role', 'Assign members to roles'. Each section addresses a user scenario, not a feature description.
Appropriate level of detail for business users. Concepts explained when needed (member roles introduction), but common UI actions like 'Select Save' are not over-explained.
Lists do not exceed 3 levels deep. Maximum nesting is 1 level (h2 sections with h3 subsections, and flat ordered/unordered lists within).

All page_link identifiers verified against front matter: tYouCw (Registration and authentication), 8oouCw (Secure pages), content_items_xp (Content items), member_roles_xp (Member roles developer page). No guessed identifiers.

All referenced pages are in the _documentation collection (same as source file). No collection parameter needed. Verified: member_roles_xp → _documentation/developers-and-admins/, content_items_xp → _documentation/business-users/, 8oouCw → _documentation/business-users/.

Anchor 'Secure content items' used on page_link to content_items_xp (lines 67, 128). This anchor references a heading on the target page. The page_link tag handles slug conversion from the heading text.
N/A — no external links on this page. All links use page_link internal references.
All link text is descriptive: 'Registration and authentication', 'pages', 'content items', 'Member roles', 'Secure pages', 'Secure content items'. No 'click here' or vague text.
Admonition tags used correctly: two {% info %}/{% endinfo %} pairs (lines 19-23 and 65-69). Tags properly matched — 2 opens, 2 closes.
N/A — no code blocks on this page. This is a business-user-facing how-to with no code examples.

All 5 image tags use correct Liquid syntax with border=true for UI screenshots: MembersList.png (line 17), MemberDetail.png (line 33), MemberRolesList.png (line 80), MemberRoleCreate.png (line 94), MemberRoleAssignMembers.png (line 119). All include title and width=400.
related_pages: ['8oouCw', 'content_items_xp', 'member_roles_xp']. All three pages are in the _documentation collection (verified via filesystem search), same as the source file.

additionalFindings.evidence: Heading hierarchy is correct: h2 ('## View member details', '## Disable or delete members', '## Member roles', '## Next steps') with h3 subsections under '## View member details' ('### External user accounts') and '## Member roles' ('### View member roles', '### Create a member role', '### Edit a member role', '### Assign members to roles'). No skipped levels.

All procedures follow the same pattern: lead-in sentence with 'To [action]:' → ordered list → result statement → optional screenshot. UI element names consistently bolded. Code names and examples consistently italicized.
All Liquid tags are properly opened and closed. {% info %}/{% endinfo %} pairs match (2 each). {% image %} and {% page_link %} tags use correct syntax with required parameters.
Front matter contains all required fields: title, persona, identifier, order, license, redirect_from, related_pages. Values are well-formed.
All sentences are clear and scannable. Procedural steps are imperative and direct. Descriptive text provides context without being verbose.

Both Attempt 1 rejections are fully resolved: (1) INV-style-031: All 'Switch to' instances replaced with 'Go to' — verified 3 occurrences on lines 76, 87, 103. (2) INV-style-036: All 'Choose' instances eliminated — the rewritten 'Assign members to roles' section uses 'select' exclusively. Zero violations remain for either invariant.

The rewritten 'Assign members to roles' section follows correct procedure patterns.
